includes: The data contract for recommendation candidate records.
Allowed candidate types, statuses, evidence types, and confidence values.
Traceability requirements linking candidates to baseline analysis, project goals, card facts, and functional roles.
The candidate lifecycle and its decision boundaries.
A first proposed candidate set for Product Owner review.
Validation expectations for recommendation candidate sets.
excludes: Decklist changes or new deck versions.

explicit_no_recommendations_boundary.statement: This file contains proposed recommendation candidates for Product Owner review. Candidates are non-actionable: no deck change is authorized by this file, no candidate is accepted or rejected, and any future deck change requires an explicit user decision, a decision log entry, and a new deck version file.

reference_conventions.pressure_point_id: Format '<analysis_id>:pressure_point:<index>' where <index> is the zero-based position in the structural_pressure_points array of the referenced analysis file. Example: 'baseline_v1.0:pressure_point:0'.
reference_conventions.open_question_id: Format '<analysis_id>:open_question:<index>' where <index> is the zero-based position in the open_questions array of the referenced analysis file. Example: 'baseline_v1.0:open_question:2'.
reference_conventions.project_goal: Verbatim goal string from project.json 'goals' or brief.json 'improvement_areas'.
reference_conventions.card_reference: Cards are referenced by Scryfall ID from workshop/card-data/cards.json, using the format 'scryfall:<id>'. Example: 'scryfall:00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' (placeholder, not a real card).

candidate_schema.description: Contract for each future entry of the 'candidates' array. Field groups: identity, traceability, evidence, content, boundary, review.

candidate_id.description: Unique ID within the recommendation set. Format 'cand-<zero-padded-number>', for example 'cand-001'.

candidate_type.description: The kind of change the candidate describes. 'knowledge_review_candidate' proposes reviewing Knowledge coverage (for example role tagging) rather than a deck change. 'role_coverage_candidate' describes a role-level gap to address without naming specific cards.

status.description: Lifecycle status. New candidates start as 'proposed'. See candidate_lifecycle for allowed transitions.

source_analysis_id.description: analysis_id of the analysis document that motivated the candidate, for example 'baseline_v1.0'. Every candidate must trace to an existing analysis.

source_pressure_point_ids.description: Pressure point IDs per reference_conventions.pressure_point_id. May be empty only for knowledge_review_candidate entries motivated by open questions alone.

related_project_goals.description: Verbatim project goal strings the candidate serves. At least one is required; candidates serving no stated goal are out of scope.

related_roles.description: role_id values affected by or motivating the candidate. Must exist in role_taxonomy.json.

related_categories.description: category_id values affected by or motivating the candidate. Must exist in role_taxonomy.json.

evidence_summary.description: One-paragraph neutral summary of why this candidate exists, grounded in cited evidence items.

evidence_items.description: Each item: { evidence_type, reference, note }. 'reference' points into the cited source (file path, ID per reference_conventions, or dated playtest note). At least one item is required.

confidence.description: Curator confidence that the candidate addresses the cited evidence. Not a card quality score.

proposed_change.description: Neutral description of the change being considered. Phrased as a candidate for evaluation, never as an instruction.

affected_cards.description: Card references per reference_conventions.card_reference. Empty for role_coverage_candidate and knowledge_review_candidate entries that do not yet name cards.

expected_impact.description: Which structural measurements (role counts, category counts, curve, color access) the change is expected to move, and in which direction.

tradeoffs.description: What the deck gives up or risks structurally if the candidate is accepted. May be empty only with reviewer-recorded justification.

risks.description: Failure modes: identity erosion (for example Myr density), goal conflicts, or Knowledge gaps that could invalidate the evidence.

constraints_checked.description: Project constraints verified before proposing, for example commander color identity, Myr typal identity preservation, and format legality per stored card facts.

is_actionable.description: Always false until an explicit user decision is recorded. Candidates are inputs to a decision, not decisions.

requires_user_decision.description: True for every candidate that would change the deck. Only the Product Owner decides.

requires_testing.description: True when acceptance should wait for playtest or simulation evidence.

creates_new_deck_version.description: True when acceptance would produce a new version file. Existing version files are never edited.

decision_log_required.description: True when acceptance or rejection must be recorded in decisions/ before any further action.

reviewer_notes.description: Dated notes from reviewers. Neutral language only.

user_decision.description: The Product Owner's recorded decision, if any. Null until decided.

decision_id.description: ID of the decision log entry in decisions/ once a decision exists. Null until decided.

follow_up_tasks.description: Task references created as a result of review, for example testing or Knowledge review tasks.

rules: Every status change is recorded in reviewer_notes with a date.
'accepted' and 'rejected' are terminal and require a recorded user_decision and decision_id.
'accepted' does not change the deck by itself: any deck change happens only through a new deck version file plus a decision log entry.
'needs_testing' candidates return to 'under_review' only with new playtest_note or simulation_result evidence attached.
No candidate may be created or promoted while this set's status is 'schema_only'.

validation_expectations: recommendation_type is either 'candidate_schema' with status 'schema_only' or 'candidate_set' with status 'candidates_proposed'.
candidates is an empty array while status is 'schema_only' and non-empty while status is 'candidates_proposed'.
Candidate records include every required field defined in candidate_schema.
candidate_id values are unique within the set.
candidate_type, status, confidence, and evidence_type values come from the allowed sets.
Candidate-set records remain status 'proposed', is_actionable false, and not accepted or rejected.
source_analysis_id references an existing analysis file's analysis_id.
source_pressure_point_ids and related_open_question_ids resolve to valid indices in the referenced analysis file.
related_roles and related_categories exist in workshop/knowledge/role_taxonomy.json.
affected_cards references resolve to Scryfall IDs present in workshop/card-data/cards.json.
Every candidate cites at least one evidence item and at least one related project goal.
Deck-altering candidates require user decision, decision log, testing, and a new deck version before implementation.
Knowledge review candidates may be non-deck-altering and may leave requires_user_decision false.
Candidates with status accepted or rejected have non-null user_decision and decision_id.
Candidate text contains no external-market, popularity, final-verdict, power-score, ranking, or tier language.
Recommendation files never modify deck, version, card-data, knowledge, analysis, decision, or report files.

candidates.evidence_summary: Baseline v1.0 flags colored-cast consistency as unverified because the deck has many colored nonland cards alongside a colorless-heavy land package. This candidate groups the stored mana-base facts for review without authorizing a land change.

evidence_items.note: Colored-cast consistency remains unverified in the structural baseline.
Future testing should examine whether colored nonland cards cast on curve.
Functional role assignments separate colored access from colorless utility production.

candidates.proposed_change: Review the balance between colored access lands and colorless utility lands, using Cascading Cataracts, Tarnished Citadel, Spire of Industry, The Mycosynth Gardens, Three Tree City, and the Urza lands as the initial evidence group.
candidates.expected_impact: Expected structural movement would be in colored_source, fixing_land, colorless_source, and utility_land_mana counts, plus future color-access testing.
tradeoffs: Changing utility land density could reduce stored colorless utility roles.
Changing colored access density could affect the existing artifact-land and utility-land mix.
risks: The baseline has not simulated colored-cast timing.
Commander identity and Myr identity must remain intact if this candidate advances.
constraints_checked: Uses only stored card facts and functional roles.
Does not edit the decklist or version files.
Does not accept a mana-base change without Product Owner decision.
reviewer_notes: 2026-07-09: Proposed for Product Owner review as a mana-base consistency candidate.
follow_up_tasks: Run future color-access testing before any deck version change.

candidates.evidence_summary: Baseline v1.0 leaves intended combo lines and burst structure open. This candidate groups existing artifact mana and tap-untap engine cards for review as an explosiveness package, without selecting a line or changing the deck.

evidence_items.note: Win-condition structure is under-described in the baseline.
Intended combo lines are not yet mapped.
Functional roles identify artifact engine pieces that can be reviewed together.

candidates.proposed_change: Review Basalt Monolith, Clock of Omens, Myr Galvanizer, Unwinding Clock, and Staff of Domination as an artifact mana and tap-untap package candidate for future testing.
candidates.expected_impact: Expected structural movement would be reviewed through mana_engine, tap_untap_engine, artifact_engine, ramp, and mana_rock role coverage.
tradeoffs: Package concentration could compete with other role coverage slots.
Engine review may expose Knowledge gaps rather than a deck construction issue.
risks: Combo mapping has not been completed.
Testing is required before treating the package as a deck-version input.
constraints_checked: Uses only existing card facts and role assignments.
Preserves Myr identity as an explicit review constraint.
Does not create or approve a deck change.
reviewer_notes: 2026-07-09: Proposed as a package candidate for later engine review and testing.
follow_up_tasks: Map artifact engine lines before any acceptance decision.

candidates.evidence_summary: Baseline v1.0 reports no anti-wipe resilience in the playable 100 and notes unresolved sideboard status. This candidate packages stored protection and resilience facts for review without moving any card.

evidence_items.note: Anti-wipe resilience is absent from the playable 100 in the baseline.
Functional roles identify protection and resilience coverage.

candidates.proposed_change: Review Darksteel Forge, Neurok Stealthsuit, and Mistrise Village as a protection and resilience package candidate, with sideboard status left unresolved until Product Owner review.
candidates.expected_impact: Expected structural movement would be in protection_and_resilience coverage, especially anti_wipe_protection and protection roles.
tradeoffs: Adding resilience emphasis could compete with engine or mana development slots.
Sideboard status must be resolved before any versioned deck action.
risks: Protection coverage may be sufficient for the intended play pattern once testing exists.
constraints_checked: Does not modify deck or version files.
Requires Product Owner decision and decision log before any future deck version.
reviewer_notes: 2026-07-09: Proposed as a resilience package candidate tied to the baseline anti-wipe pressure point.
follow_up_tasks: Resolve sideboard status before considering a versioned deck action.

candidates.evidence_summary: Baseline v1.0 notes that win-condition structure may be a Knowledge gap rather than a deck gap. This candidate asks for role coverage review before any deck-changing recommendation is considered.

evidence_items.note: No cards are currently carrying finisher, combo_piece, combo_enabler, or alternate_win_condition roles in the baseline.
Aetherflux Reservoir support remains an open question.

evidence_items.reference: combo_piece, combo_enabler, combo_payoff, finisher, damage_payoff, scaling_threat
evidence_items.note: The relevant role vocabulary exists and can be reviewed against current assignments.

candidates.proposed_change: Review combo and win-condition role coverage for Walking Ballista, Aetherflux Reservoir, Basalt Monolith, Clock of Omens, and Myr Galvanizer before making any deck construction candidate from the baseline pressure point.
candidates.expected_impact: Expected movement would be Knowledge-layer clarity for combo_and_win_conditions and engine_and_scaling roles, not a decklist change.
tradeoffs: Knowledge review may delay deck construction candidates.
Role updates, if any, must be handled in a later scoped task.
risks: Current role assignments may be intentionally conservative.
Card interactions still need a separate synergy-mapping step.
constraints_checked: Does not edit role assignments in this task.
Does not create a deck-changing recommendation.
Uses existing role taxonomy IDs only.
reviewer_notes: 2026-07-09: Proposed as a Knowledge review candidate only.
follow_up_tasks: Open a later Knowledge review task if Product Owner wants role coverage revisited.

candidates.evidence_summary: Baseline v1.0 identifies card advantage as comparatively small. This candidate records a role-coverage review path for consistency without naming a deck change.

evidence_items.note: Card advantage has 11 cards and 6 primary-role cards in the baseline.
The taxonomy separates draw, selection, filtering, and tutoring roles for later review.
Consistency is a stated project goal.

candidates.proposed_change: Review card advantage and selection role coverage as a consistency candidate before naming any specific add, cut, or swap candidate.
candidates.expected_impact: Expected movement would be clearer coverage targets for card_advantage and selection_and_tutoring before any future deck-version candidate.
tradeoffs: Role-level review may not identify a deck action.
Future card-level candidates would need separate evidence and Product Owner review.
risks: Current draw and selection counts may be adequate for the intended play pattern.
No playtest evidence exists yet for consistency failures.
constraints_checked: No real card is proposed by this candidate.
No decklist or version file is changed.
Future deck-changing candidates must be created separately.
reviewer_notes: 2026-07-09: Proposed as a role-coverage candidate tied to consistency.
follow_up_tasks: Create a later card-level candidate only if Product Owner requests it.

candidates.evidence_summary: Baseline v1.0 identifies recovery as artifact-directed. This candidate groups artifact recursion and utility-land recovery cards for review while preserving the project boundary that no deck action is approved.

evidence_items.note: Recursion is exclusively artifact-directed in the baseline.
Functional roles identify the recovery channels already represented.
Resilience and Myr identity are stated project goals.

candidates.proposed_change: Review Myr Retriever, Academy Ruins, Buried Ruin, Inventors' Fair, and Urza's Cave as an artifact recursion and recovery package candidate for future resilience review.
candidates.expected_impact: Expected structural movement would be reviewed through artifact_recursion, graveyard_card_access, utility_land, and board_presence coverage.
tradeoffs: Recovery focus could compete with proactive engine or board-presence slots if advanced later.
Non-artifact recovery remains outside this candidate.
risks: The artifact-directed recovery shape may be intentional for the deck identity.
No testing exists yet to show recovery failure frequency.
constraints_checked: Preserves Myr identity as a review constraint.
Requires Product Owner decision before any versioned action.
reviewer_notes: 2026-07-09: Proposed as an artifact recovery package candidate.
follow_up_tasks: Compare recovery package behavior during future testing.
